lessons.title: Hailing an Auto & the Meter
lessons.scene: You're standing on the roadside in Jayanagar. An empty auto slows down beside you. The driver leans out.

steps.speaker: Auto Driver
steps.english: Where do you want to go, sir?

steps.prompt: Tell him you want to go to Lalbagh, and to use the meter

choices.english: I need to go to Lalbagh. Put the meter on.
I need to go to Lalbagh. No meter.
choices.hint: Saying 'meter beda' invites a fixed (often inflated) price. Insist on 'meter haaki' (put the meter).
You're asking HIM for directions! State your destination with '-ge' (to): 'Lalbagh-ge'.

correctReaction.speaker: Auto Driver
correctReaction.english: You'll come by meter? Okay, sit, sir.

steps.grammarTip: '-beku' attached to a verb means 'must / have to': 'hogu' (go) → 'hogbeku' (have to go). Combine with the destination + '-ge': 'Lalbagh-ge hogbeku' = (I) have to go to Lalbagh.

steps.speaker: Auto Driver
steps.kannada: Saar, ee time traffic jaasti. Swalpa late aagbahudu.
steps.script: ಸಾರ್, ಈ ಟೈಮ್ ಟ್ರಾಫಿಕ್ ಜಾಸ್ತಿ. ಸ್ವಲ್ಪ ಲೇಟ್ ಆಗ್ಬಹುದು.
steps.pronunciation: saar, ee time TRAA-phik JAAS-ti. SWAL-pa late AAG-ba-hu-du
steps.english: Sir, traffic is heavy this time. It might get a bit late.

steps.prompt: Say it's okay, you're not in a hurry

choices.english: It's fine, I'm not in a hurry.
Fast, fast! I'm in a big hurry!
choices.hint: He can't beat traffic, and rushing him won't help. A calm 'parvagilla' keeps the ride pleasant.

choices.english: In that case I'll get off here.
choices.hint: No need to abandon the ride over a little traffic! Just reassure him: 'avasara illa'.

wrongReaction.speaker: Auto Driver
wrongReaction.english: Nothing can be done in traffic, sir.

steps.grammarTip: '-bahudu' means 'may / might': 'aagu' (happen) → 'aagbahudu' (it may happen). And '-ona' makes a friendly 'let's...': 'hogona' (let's go), 'tinnona' (let's eat).

lessons.title: Directing the Route & Paying
lessons.scene: You're nearly there. The driver needs to know exactly where to turn and stop near the Lalbagh west gate.

steps.speaker: Auto Driver
steps.english: Sir, where ahead? Shall I go straight?

steps.prompt: Tell him to go straight and then turn left at the signal

choices.english: Go straight, turn left at the signal.

choices.hint: You're not there yet — stopping now strands you. Guide him: 'nera hogi' then 'edagade tirugi'.
That's the wrong way — 'balagade' is right. You want 'edagade' (left).

wrongReaction.speaker: Auto Driver
wrongReaction.english: Left or right sir? I got confused.

steps.grammarTip: '-alli' means 'at / in': 'signal-alli' = at the signal, 'mane-alli' = at home. It attaches straight onto the place word and is one of the most useful endings for directions.

steps.prompt: The gate is just ahead — ask him to stop there

choices.hint: But the gate is right here! Tell him to stop near it: 'gate hattira nilisi'.
That sails right past your stop. Use 'nilisi' (stop) at the gate.

correctReaction.speaker: Auto Driver
correctReaction.english: Done sir. It's eighty rupees on the meter.

wrongReaction.speaker: Auto Driver
wrongReaction.english: Tell me where to stop, sir.

steps.grammarTip: 'Ee' = this (near), 'aa' = that (far): 'ee gate' (this gate) vs 'aa gate' (that gate). Pointing words like these make directions crystal clear to a driver.

steps.speaker: You
steps.kannada: Ido togoli, chillare ide. Dhanyavada saar!
steps.script: ಇದೋ ತೊಗೊಳಿ, ಚಿಲ್ಲರೆ ಇದೆ. ಧನ್ಯವಾದ ಸಾರ್!
steps.pronunciation: i-do to-GO-li, CHIL-la-re i-de. DHAN-ya-vaa-da saar
steps.english: Here, take it, I have exact change. Thank you sir!

lessons.title: Asking the Auto to Wait (Round Trip)
lessons.scene: You need to quickly drop a parcel at a friend's flat two streets away and come straight back home. You stop an auto and want it to wait a few minutes and bring you back.

steps.speaker: Auto Driver
steps.english: Come sir, where do you want to go?

steps.prompt: Say it's close by, ask him to wait ten minutes and bring you back

choices.kannada: Hattira-ne ide. Hattu nimisha kaayri, vaapas karkond banni.
choices.script: ಹತ್ತಿರಾನೇ ಇದೆ. ಹತ್ತು ನಿಮಿಷ ಕಾಯ್ರಿ, ವಾಪಸ್ ಕರ್ಕೊಂಡ್ ಬನ್ನಿ.
choices.pronunciation: HAT-ti-raa-ne i-de. HAT-tu ni-MI-sha KAAY-ri, VAA-pas KAR-kond BAN-ni
choices.english: It's close by. Wait ten minutes and bring me back.

choices.kannada: Doora ide. Kaayodu beda, hogtaa irona.
choices.script: ದೂರ ಇದೆ. ಕಾಯೋದು ಬೇಡ, ಹೋಗ್ತಾ ಇರೋಣ.
choices.pronunciation: DOO-ra i-de. KAA-yo-du BEH-da, HOG-taa i-RO-na
choices.english: It's far. No waiting, let's just keep going.
choices.hint: But you need a round trip! Tell him it's near and to wait: 'hattira ide, kaayri, vaapas karkond banni'.
That sends HIM away! You want him to wait and take YOU back: 'kaayri, vaapas karkond banni'.

correctReaction.speaker: Auto Driver
correctReaction.kannada: Waiting-ge swalpa jaasti aagutte saar, parvagilla-na?
correctReaction.script: ವೈಟಿಂಗ್‌ಗೆ ಸ್ವಲ್ಪ ಜಾಸ್ತಿ ಆಗುತ್ತೆ ಸಾರ್, ಪರವಾಗಿಲ್ಲ-ನಾ?
correctReaction.pronunciation: WAI-ting-ge SWAL-pa JAAS-ti AA-gut-te saar, par-VAA-gil-la-naa
correctReaction.english: The waiting will cost a bit more, sir — is that okay?

wrongReaction.speaker: Auto Driver
wrongReaction.kannada: Naanu kaayli beka, beda? Sariyaagi heli saar.
wrongReaction.script: ನಾನು ಕಾಯ್ಲಿ ಬೇಕಾ, ಬೇಡ? ಸರಿಯಾಗಿ ಹೇಳಿ ಸಾರ್.
wrongReaction.pronunciation: NAA-nu KAAY-li BEH-kaa, BEH-da? sa-ri-YAA-gi HEH-li saar
wrongReaction.english: Should I wait or not? Tell me properly, sir.

steps.grammarTip: 'Vaapas' (back/return) is the everyday word for any round trip — 'vaapas banni' (come back), 'vaapas kodi' (give it back). 'Karkond banni' literally is 'taking (me), come' — i.e. bring me along.

steps.prompt: Agree, and ask how much extra he'll charge for waiting

choices.english: That's fine, how much extra will it be?

choices.hint: Waiting time fairly costs a little more. Accept it and just ask the amount: 'eshtu jaasti aagutte?'.
'Doora' (far) asks distance, not cost. For the extra charge ask 'eshtu jaasti?' (how much more?).

correctReaction.speaker: Auto Driver
correctReaction.kannada: Meter mele nalvattu jaasti haakdini saar. Kutkolli.
correctReaction.script: ಮೀಟರ್ ಮೇಲೆ ನಲವತ್ತು ಜಾಸ್ತಿ ಹಾಕ್ದೀನಿ ಸಾರ್. ಕೂತ್ಕೊಳ್ಳಿ.
correctReaction.pronunciation: MEE-tar ME-le na-LA-vat-tu JAAS-ti HAAK-dee-ni saar. KOOT-kol-li
correctReaction.english: I'll add forty over the meter, sir. Get in.

wrongReaction.speaker: Auto Driver
wrongReaction.english: In that case I can't wait, sir.

steps.grammarTip: 'Mele' = 'on top of / over': 'meter mele' = over (and above) the meter. Useful for any add-on: 'adara mele' (on top of that). 'Eshtu jaasti aagutte?' = 'how much more will it come to?' is your go-to for any surcharge.

steps.speaker: You
steps.kannada: Sari saar, hattu nimisha-dalli vaapas bartini. Kaayri.
steps.script: ಸರಿ ಸಾರ್, ಹತ್ತು ನಿಮಿಷದಲ್ಲಿ ವಾಪಸ್ ಬರ್ತೀನಿ. ಕಾಯ್ರಿ.
steps.pronunciation: SA-ri saar, HAT-tu ni-MI-sha-dal-li VAA-pas BAR-tee-ni. KAAY-ri
steps.english: Okay sir, I'll be back in ten minutes. Please wait.
